folio.label: 117r
folio.l: Wel betir than Ianuarie , hire owen make
For in a &lbar;re , she had told hi~ alle
Of this matere , how he werken shalle
And thus I lete hi~ sitte , vp oon this Perie
And Ianuarie and May , romyngö ful merye
¢ Brigút was the day , and blew the firmament
Phebus hatú of gold , hise stremes doun sent´
That gladen euäy flour , witú hise warmnes
He was that tyme , in gemine as I gesse
But litel from his , declinaciouö
Of Cancer , Iouis exaltaciou~
And so byfel , in that brigút morwe tyde
That in that gardeyn , in the ferther syde
Pluto that is kyngö , of fayrie
And many a lady , in his companye
Folwyngö his wyf , the Quene of Proserpyne
Ecú after other , as rigút as a lyue
While that she gadered , floures in the mede
In Claudian , ©e moun the stories rede
How in his grisly Cart , he hire fette
¢ The kyngö of fayrie , doun hi~ sette
Vp on a benche , of torues faire and grene
And rigút a noon , thus seide he to the Quene
Now wyf q¶ he , there may no wygút sey nay
The ex¹ience , so preeuetú it euäy day
The tresou~ , whicú that wo~man dotú to man
Ten hondred thou©sand , wel tellen I can
Notable , of ©oure vntrewthe , and brotilnes
O noble Salamou~ , richest of Ryches
Fulfilled of sapience , and of worldly glorie
Ful worthy ben thyne wordes , to memorie
To euäy wygút , that wyt and resou~ can
Thus preisetú he ©et , the bounte of man
Amongö a thou©sand men , ©et fond I one
But of wo~men alle , ©et fond I noone
Thus seitú the kyngö , that knowetú ©oure wykkednes
And Iúc filius Sirac , as I gesse
Ne speketú of ©ow , but selde reuäence
A wylde fyre , and corupte pestilence
So fal vp oon ©oure bodyes , ©et to nygút
Ne se ©e nougút , this honåable knygút
By cause allas , that he is blynd and old
His owen man , shal make hi~ cokewold
Lo where he syt , the lechour oon the tree
Now wol I gunte , of my mageste
